channel.title: Microsoft Learn - in Intune
channel.description: The latest updates from Microsoft Learn in Intune

item.title: Remote Device Actions - Wipe, Lock, Locate, and More - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how to use Microsoft Intune to remotely manage, wipe, lock, restart, and secure Android, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, Windows, and ChromeOS devices. Learn about available remote actions, prerequisites, and bulk actions for IT admins.

item.title: Expedite Policies for Windows Quality Updates - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use expedite policies in Microsoft Intune to quickly deploy a specific Windows security update, bypass deferrals, and monitor installation status across devices.

item.title: Use Multi Admin Approval in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure Multi Admin Approval to protect your tenant against the use of compromised administrative accounts in Intune.

item.title: Use role-based access control (RBAC) and scope tags for distributed IT - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use role-based access control (RBAC) and scope tags to filter configuration profiles to specific roles.

item.title: Remote Device Action: Collect Diagnostics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to collect diagnostics with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use reusable groups of settings policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage groups of settings for Intune profiles as a single object and then add that settings group object to multiple profile instances. Later changes you make to the settings groups automatically apply to each profile that includes the reusable settings group.

item.title: Get support in the Microsoft Intune admin center - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get online and telephone support for Microsoft Intune and other device management cloud services from within the Microsoft Intune admin center.

item.title: What's new in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find out what's new in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Frequently asked questions about MAM and app protection - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article provides answers to some frequently asked questions on Intune mobile application management (MAM) and Intune app protection.

item.title: Set up local admin account creation and password management for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up macOS account configuration with LAPS through automatic device enrollment for macOS devices in Intune.

item.title: Intune Graph API - Reports and Properties - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Intune reports and properties provided via Graph API.

item.title: Manage Windows Update Ring Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Windows Update ring policies for Windows devices, how to create and manage them, and improve update deployment.

item.title: Planning guide to move to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan, design, implement, adopt, and move to Microsoft Intune. Get guidance and advice to determine goals, use-case scenarios and requirements, and create rollout and communication plans, support, testing, and validation plans.

item.title: Assign device profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Microsoft Intune admin center to assign device configuration profiles and policies to users and devices. Learn how to exclude groups from a profile assignment in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Questions with policies and profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Common questions, answers, and scenarios with device policies and profiles in Microsoft Intune. Learn more about profile changes not applying to users or devices, how long it takes for new policies to deploy, which settings apply when there are conflicts, what happens when you delete or remove a profile, and more.

item.title: Windows Update Management Overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage Windows updates with Intune. Control update settings, define rollout strategies, and ensure consistent device security across your organization.

item.title: Configure device configuration profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add or configure a device configuration profile in Microsoft Intune. Select the platform type, configure the settings, and add a scope tag. Learn more about applicability rules, and the policy refresh cycle times.

item.title: Reports for Windows Feature Update Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the reports available for Windows feature update policies in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Query - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use device query in Microsoft Intune to get on-demand device state, run Kusto Query Language (KQL) queries, and troubleshoot devices.

item.title: Configure Windows Driver Update Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to create, approve, deploy, and pause Windows driver updates using Intune policies to keep Windows devices current and stable.

item.title: Manage Windows Driver Updates - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage Windows driver updates using Intune driver update policies to keep Windows devices current and stable.

item.title: See device configuration policies with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See and manage the device configuration policy details in Microsoft Intune. Look at a graphical chart of the number of devices assigned to a policy, and see which devices have policies assigned or deployed. Can also troubleshoot policies that have conflict settings.

item.title: Device Query for Multiple Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use device query for multiple devices in Microsoft Intune to run KQL queries, analyze inventory trends, and start querying your managed fleet now.

item.title: Zero Trust with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune contributes to a strong Zero Trust strategy and architecture by managing and securing endpoints that access organizational resources.

item.title: Centrally manage Admin Tasks - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Centrally manage admin tasks in Microsoft Intune. Use the unified Admin tasks view to organize and act on administrative tasks from Device Offboarding, Endpoint Privilege Management, and more.

item.title: Use Hotpatch With Windows Quality Updates - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how hotpatch works with Windows quality update policies in Microsoft Intune to install eligible security updates without requiring an immediate device restart.

item.title: Endpoint management services and solutions at Microsoft - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune is a family of on-premises products and cloud services. It includes Intune, Configuration Manager, co-management, Endpoint Analytics, Windows Autopilot, and the admin center to manage cloud devices and on on-premises.

item.title: Control AI features on Android Enterprise devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Using Microsoft Intune, you can manage and restrict AI usage on Android devices enrolled in Intune. This guide provides lists the steps in Intune to block AI apps, websites, screen-driven experiences, on-device AI services, and OEM-specific AI features. You can manage Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Samsung Galaxy AI, claude.ai, ChatGPT, and more.

item.title: Reports for Windows Quality Update Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the reports available for Windows quality update policies in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Reports for Windows Driver Update Policies reports - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Intune reports to monitor Windows driver updates for devices assigned to Windows Driver update policies.

item.title: Reports for Windows Update Ring Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the reports available for Windows update ring policies in Intune. Discover how to access and interpret these reports to monitor update deployments.

item.title: Security Copilot in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: You can use Security Copilot to get information about your Intune data, including devices, apps, policies, and groups managed in Intune. You can also compare policies, get device specific details, and get target info for policies.

item.title: Create a policy using settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to configure thousands of settings for Windows 10/11, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, and Android client devices, including Microsoft Office apps, Microsoft Edge, administrative templates (ADMX), and more. Add these settings in a device configuration profile to secure devices, and control different programs and features. Use Microsoft Copilot to get What If analysis, and learn more about each setting.

item.title: Use the Change Review Agent - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the Change Review Agent in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use the Policy Configuration Agent to create policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the Policy Configuration Agent in Microsoft Intune to create policies. You can create policies on files you uploaded, natural language prompts, or industry baselines. The Policy Configuration Agent is a feature of Security Copilot in Intune.

item.title: Learn about Policy Configuration Agent and set it up - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Policy Configuration Agent in Microsoft Intune, its prerequisites, how it works, and how to set it up. The agent is a generative AI feature that helps create Intune policies based on uploaded documents or benchmarks. The Policy Configuration Agent is a feature of Security Copilot in Intune.

item.title: Change Review Agent Overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Change Review Agent in Microsoft Intune, its prerequisites, and how it works.

item.title: Use the Device Offboarding Agent - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the Device Offboarding Agent in Microsoft Intune to identify and offboard devices that are no longer in use.

item.title: Microsoft Security Copilot agents in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Security Copilot agents in Microsoft Intune

item.title: Explore Intune data with natural language and take action - Microsoft Intune
item.description: In Microsoft Intune, use Explorer to query your data in natural language and run built-in queries that match your request. Copilot summarizes the results, and provides recommendations and actions based on the query results. You can also create policies that target users and groups in the query results. Use this feature to explore your Intune data, troubleshoot issues, and create custom reports.

item.title: Get Started with the Device Offboarding Agent - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Device Offboarding Agent in Microsoft Intune, its prerequisites, how it works, and how to configure it.

item.title: Copilot in Intune shows device information and helps troubleshoot - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Security Copilot in Intune can help you get information about your devices, compare devices, and get error information. Use this information to help you manage and troubleshoot device issues.

item.title: Security Copilot Agents in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how Microsoft Security Copilot enhances Microsoft Intune through AI-powered security agents. Learn about available agents and explore their capabilities.

item.title: Use Compatibility Reports for Windows Updates in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use compatibility reports for Windows devices in Intune to identify app and driver risks before deploying feature updates or update rings.

item.title: Security Copilot in Intune features overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Security Copilot in Intune is an AI platform. It can help you create policies, get information about existing policies, and show more details on specific settings, including their impacts on users and devices. You can also use Copilot to troubleshoot device issues and query devices.

item.title: Vulnerability Remediation Agent Overview and Set Up - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Vulnerability Remediation Agent in Microsoft Intune, its prerequisites, how it works, and how to set it up. The agent uses AI to identify and prioritize vulnerability remediation based on Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management data.

item.title: What is Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune manages users and devices, simplifies app management and automated policy deployment, and integrates with mobile threat defense. It connects to Managed Google Play, Apple tokens and certificates, and Teamviewer for remote assistance. Can use MDM or MAM to protect data, configure devices, and simplify access to company resources.

item.title: Use the Vulnerability Remediation Agent to Manage Vulnerabilities - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the Vulnerability Remediation Agent in Microsoft Intune to identify, prioritize, and remediate security vulnerabilities across your managed devices.

item.title: FAQ for Microsoft Copilot in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get answers to common questions when using Copilot in Microsoft Intune, including accessing Copilot, licensing, and more.

item.title: In development - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes Microsoft Intune features that are in development.

item.title: Deployment guide for Linux device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use our platform deployment guide to set up Linux device management in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Linux device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Linux devices in Intune using the Intune app. Set an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Platforms and policy types supported by assignment filters - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn which apps, compliance policies, and device configuration profiles and their platforms support assignment filters in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add and Assign Win32 Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add, assign, and manage Win32 apps with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Learn about the Microsoft Tunnel VPN solution for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway, a VPN server for Intune that runs on Linux. With Microsoft Tunnel, cloud-based devices you manage with Intune can reach your on-premises infrastructure.

item.title: Prerequisites the Microsoft Tunnel VPN for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review the prerequisites for installation and use of the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway. These include Linux servers, network, and firewall configurations.

item.title: Use Microsoft Tunnel VPN with Android devices that don't enroll with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add support for Mobile Application Management (MAM) for Android to the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway. Tunnel support for MAM expands access to your organizational resources for devices that can't or haven't enrolled with Microsoft Intune

item.title: Microsoft Intune App SDK for Android Developer Integration and Testing Guide - App Participation Features - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand App participation features when incorporating Intune mobile app management (MAM) into your Android app.

item.title: Encrypt Windows devices with BitLocker using Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune policy to manage BitLocker encryption on Windows devices, including silent encryption and Personal Data Encryption.

item.title: Manage Apple Volume-Purchased Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to sync apps you bought in volume through Apple Business Manager with Microsoft Intune. Then manage and track these apps on iOS/iPadOS and macOS devices.

item.title: Apple device restriction settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add, configure, or create settings on iOS, iPadOS, and macOS devices to restrict features in Microsoft Intune. Create password requirements, control the locked screen, use built-in apps, add restricted or approved apps, handle bluetooth devices, connect to the cloud for backup and storage, enable kiosk mode, add domains, and control how users interact with the Safari web browser.

item.title: Configure Update Policies for Apple Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure software update policies for Apple devices using Apple's Declarative Device Management (DDM) model. Improve security, reduce user disruption, and ensure compliance with reliable, automated updates across your organization.

item.title: What Is App Management in Microsoft Intune? - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about client app management capabilities by platform for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Kiosk settings for Windows in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure your Windows 10/11 client devices as single-app and multi-app kiosks, customize the start menu, add apps, show the task bar, and configure a web browser in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Using Azure Virtual Desktop multi-session with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Guidelines for using Azure Virtual Desktop multi-session with Microsoft Intune

item.title: Add Microsoft Store Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about adding Microsoft Store apps to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device restriction settings for Windows devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See a list of all the settings and their descriptions for creating device restrictions on Windows 10/11 client devices. Use these settings in a configuration profile to control screenshots, password requirements, kiosk settings, apps in the store, Microsoft Edge browser, Microsoft Defender, access to the cloud, start menu, and more in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure a tenant-wide Windows Hello for Business policy with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Apply policy for Windows Hello for Business on devices at the time they enroll with Microsoft Intune

item.title: Update Rings Policy Settings - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Reference article that lists all the settings for Windows Update rings.

item.title: Add Microsoft 365 Apps to Windows Devices Using Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how you can use Microsoft Intune to install Microsoft 365 apps on Windows devices.

item.title: Import custom and third-party partner ADMX templates in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: You can add, upload, or import custom and third-party partner ADMX and ADML files in Microsoft Intune. When they're imported, create a device configuration profile and assign the profile to your Windows 10/11 client devices.

item.title: Remote Device Action: Find lost devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Locate lost or stolen devices by using the locate device feature in Microsoft Intune. Get details on security and privacy information when using the locate device action.

item.title: Get started with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See an overview of the steps to start using Intune. Plan your move and deployment of Intune, determine your licensing needs and any platform requirements, use compliance and Conditional Access, deploy apps, create device configuration profiles, and enroll your devices to be managed. Get more information on mobile application management for BYOD or personal devices.

item.title: Device features and settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview of the different Microsoft Intune device profiles. Get info on GPO, features, restrictions, email, wifi, VPN, education, certificates, upgrade Windows 10/11, BitLocker and Microsoft Defender, and custom device configuration settings in the Microsoft Intune admin center. Use these profiles to manage and protect data and devices in your company.

item.title: Manage account protection settings with endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune endpoint security account protection policies to protect the identity and accounts of users.

item.title: Create device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create device compliance policies for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use ADMX templates on Windows devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the settings catalog to configure administrative templates (ADMX) in Microsoft Intune on Windows 10/11 client devices. You can configure some Control Panel features, Start menu and task bar notifications, event log service, prevent IIS installation, and more.

item.title: Remote Device Action: Disable Activation Lock - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Microsoft Intune to disable Activation Lock on Apple devices.

item.title: Use Microsoft Intune policies to manage macOS software updates - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to manage system updates for supervised macOS devices.

item.title: Manage and secure user and group identities overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get an overview of the concepts and features you should know when managing identities in Microsoft Intune. Use existing users and groups, control access using RBAC, establish user affinity, and secure and authenticate users.

item.title: Add custom settings for Windows devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add or create a custom profile to use the OMA-URI settings for devices running Windows 10/11 client in Microsoft Intune. Use a custom profile to add custom settings.

item.title: Remote Device Action: Reset Passcode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to reset a passcode with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Migrate your imported group policy to a policy in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: After you import your Windows group policy objects in Microsoft Intune, use the migrate feature to transfer your GPOs to a Settings Catalog policy. This policy uses your imported GPOs, and can be assigned to users and devices managed by your organizations.

item.title: Windows Delivery Optimization settings for Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Delivery Optimization settings for Windows devices that you can deploy using Intune.

item.title: Get started with macOS endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune guide to set up and configure macOS devices from setup to creating policies and enrolling devices. You secure your corporate and organization-owned endpoints that are enrolled in Intune, and then deploy at scale with Apple Business Manager or Apple School Manager.

item.title: Configure Rollout Options for Feature Update Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure rollout options in feature update policies to control when Windows feature updates become available to devices and deploy updates gradually.

item.title: Deploy policy for Windows Hello to groups of Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use a Microsoft Intune profile for Identity protection configure Windows Hello for Business on Windows devices.

item.title: Deployment guide for Windows device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use our platform deployment guide to set up Windows device management in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add preference file settings to macOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add an xml or plist file that includes key information about your app. Use a preference file device configuration profile to change key information in the property list file, and assign it to your macOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: eSIM configuration of a download server - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about configuration of an eSIM Download Server from Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Admin guide and checklist for macOS software updates - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Guidance and advice for administrators that create and manage software updates for macOS devices using Microsoft Intune. Use this article to help manage software updates on your organization-owned devices, including how to create policies, manage updates, and review update status.

item.title: Add custom settings to Apple devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Export iOS, iPadOS, and macOS settings from Apple Configurator or Apple Profile Manager tools, and then import these settings into Microsoft Intune. These settings can create, use, and control custom settings and features on iOS, iPadOS, and macOS devices. This custom profile can then be assigned or distributed to iOS, iPadOS, and macOS devices in your organization to create a baseline or standard.

item.title: Configure security, email, VPN, and Wi-Fi device configuration profiles - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 4 to deploy device configuration profiles as part of the minimum set of policies for your devices using Microsoft Intune. The starting point is to enable the firewall, install AV, scan for malware, install software updates, create a strong PIN policy, and create email, VPN, and Wi-Fi device configuration profiles.

item.title: Configure endpoint protection on macOS devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune to configure macOS devices use the built-in firewall to allow or block specific apps or to use stealth mode, to use Gatekeeper to determine where apps install, and to use FileVault disk encryption.

item.title: Remote Device Action: Remove Passcode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to remove a passcode with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Linux device compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the device compliance settings for Linux that you can manage with Microsoft Intune compliance policies.

item.title: Configure Endpoint protection settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create Endpoint protection settings when you create a macOS or Windows device profile in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: macOS extension settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add, configure, or create settings on macOS devices to use system extensions and kernel extensions. Also, allow users to override approved extensions, allow all extensions from a team identifier, or allow specific extensions or apps in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Update your workloads to support cloud-native endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: To support hybrid and remote workers, convert or migrate your workloads to support cloud-native endpoints. This planning guide focuses on deploying apps and updates with Intune, moving from Group Policy Objects, and using Windows Autopilot.

item.title: Common Education OneDrive Known Folder Move configuration - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about common OneDrive Known Folder Move configuration used by Education organizations in Intune.
